data.body:
Follow-up assessment for approved recommendations 5, 6, and 9: measured remediation required before enabling gates.

Complexity C901:
- Threshold 10: 154 current failures.
- Threshold 15: 52 failures.
- Threshold 20: 24 failures.
- Threshold 25: 13 failures.
- Threshold 30: 9 failures; maximum observed complexity is 44.
Recommendation: do not use per-file ignores because they allow new complex functions in exempt files, and do not add 52 inline noqas. Add a checked-in symbol-level baseline keyed by path, function, and current score. Gate all unlisted functions at 15 and fail if any baselined score increases or a new symbol appears; baseline entries may only shrink. Prioritize the nine functions above 30 for refactoring, then drain 25, 20, and 15 bands.

Mypy:
- no_implicit_optional: zero failures, enable immediately.
- strict_equality: zero failures, enable immediately.
- warn_redundant_casts: one failure, remove the cast in llm/workflow/editor.py, then enable.
- warn_unused_ignores: 23 failures, mostly stale reconciler ignores plus two workflow/schema ignores; remove or revalidate them, then enable.
- disallow_any_generics: 831 failures, so global enablement is not viable. Even the existing strict graph and grounding packages have only nine failures; fix those nine and add disallow_any_generics to their existing override. Continue package-by-package.
- disallow_untyped_defs: 585 whole-tree failures, but reducer, store, snapshot, schemas, review_bot, and engine_support total only ten failures. Annotate those ten definitions and promote those packages into the existing shrink-only strict override before tackling llm, commands, and engine.

Ruff additions:
- ASYNC has zero production findings and can be enabled for src now. Tests have one ASYNC110; fix or narrowly exempt that test.
- DTZ has three production findings; fix timezone handling in validate_checks and security_pin, then enable.
- RUF has 87 RUF100 stale-noqa findings under the full rule set, 55 mechanical findings, and 76 ambiguous-Unicode findings. Remove the 87 stale noqas after review; auto-fix/safely review the 55 mechanical cases; defer RUF001/2/3 with explicit code-level ignores because many symbols are intentional UI/prose. Then enable the selected RUF subset, not the whole family.
- S has 160 subprocess findings, 54 silent-exception findings, 32 production asserts, 20 URL-open findings, and a smaller 35-item high-risk set including tar extraction, XML parsing, MD5, public binds, chmod, temp paths, and timezone/randomness checks. Triage and remediate the 35 high-risk findings first. Enable proven individual S codes. Do not globally enable S603/S607 until subprocess execution is centralized behind the existing process seam or each call is validated; do not blanket-ignore whole files. Review S110/S112 for logging or justified inline suppression. Convert runtime asserts to explicit errors or justify invariant-only cases.
- Tests should permanently ignore S101 and likely S603/S607 at test scope; otherwise over 2,000 test assertions plus about 900 subprocess findings swamp the signal.

Semgrep:
- The local binary cannot validate or scan even with metrics and version checks disabled because its X509 trust store initializes with no anchors. Recommendation 2 must first pin the container scanner and add a build-time offline validate plus known-positive/known-negative fixture scan.
- Add broader vendored rules in advisory, diff-scoped mode first. Capture a main-branch SARIF baseline and promote rules individually only after false-positive review. No production cleanup is required before advisory rollout; blocking promotion requires a clean or explicitly adjudicated rule baseline.

No files were modified.
